corpus.title: A Thriving and Sustainable Built Environment — Richmond Civic Hackathon
corpus.description: Research corpus on Richmond's built environment problem space: infrastructure project transparency, fleet operations visibility, and resident awareness of public works construction and services.

files.title: A Thriving and Sustainable Built Environment — Main Entry Point
files.summary: Primary entry point for hackathon teams. Covers the three core questions, decision phases (0–9), guardrails, repo map, and Hackbot introduction. Read first for orientation.

files.title: Quick Start — Minimal Path for Built Environment Teams
files.summary: Minimal path from landing to MVP scope for built environment teams. Prioritized for teams with 30 minutes or less before they need to make decisions.
recommended_audience: teams that need a clear starting point

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot: personality, boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation instructions, guardrails, and decision logic. AI agents should read this before acting.

files.title: Claude Agent Configuration
files.summary: Behavioral configuration for Claude when acting as Hackbot. Defines role, operating rules, preferred interaction patterns, and output formats for this pillar.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Self-describing guide for humans and AI agents on how to navigate and use the research corpus. Covers canonical source of truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior requirements.
recommended_audience: hackathon participants unfamiliar with the corpus

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports, metadata, indexes, and navigation files. Required reading before modifying the corpus structure.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus updates

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Boot prompt for initializing Hackbot with full context for this pillar. Use this to start a fresh Hackbot session with all relevant context pre-loaded.

files.title: Evidence Log
files.summary: Verified factual claims about Richmond's built environment with official source URLs and verification status. Use to confirm specific facts before citing them.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the A Thriving and Sustainable Built Environment pillar: mission, scope, key problem areas, and how infrastructure transparency fits the Richmond Civic Hackathon.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Alignment document connecting this pillar to the Richmond MAP (Municipal Action Plan) framework. Documents how built environment work connects to citywide goals.

files.title: Blue Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, aspirational problem statements for the built environment pillar. Starting point for teams exploring problems before narrowing to targeted statements.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements
files.summary: Scored, prioritized problem statements for the two main opportunities: Transportation Project Visibility (25/32 — Strong) and Fleet Operations Visibility (22/32 — Needs work). Read before choosing an MVP direction.

files.title: Data Source Index
files.summary: Index of all data sources catalogued for the built environment pillar. Cross-references source_inventory.csv with human-readable descriptions and access notes.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: CSV inventory of verified data sources with access mode, update frequency, key fields, and notes. Machine-readable complement to 02_data/00_index.md.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Synthesized scan of comparable civic infrastructure tools from other cities. Complements E-section research reports with curated benchmark examples.
recommended_audience: teams researching what has been built elsewhere

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon continuation plan identifying partners, artifacts worth continuing, and next steps for built environment projects beyond the weekend.

files.title: DPW Data Landscape
files.summary: Synthesized landscape of Richmond DPW's publicly available data assets. Maps data availability to potential MVP use cases.

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Synthesized prototype recommendations for the built environment pillar. Provides ranked MVP options with rationale grounded in research.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Synthesized user journeys for key personas including the concerned resident, DPW supervisor, and DPW communications staff. Complements B-section research reports.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Describes candidate MVP shapes for the built environment pillar with trade-offs, scope, and technical approaches for each. Read before Phase 4 opportunity framing.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture recommendations for built environment MVPs. Covers ArcGIS integration patterns, geocoding options, mapping libraries, and fallback strategies.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Practical demo advice for built environment civic prototypes. Covers narrative structure, judge-facing framing, and how to present synthetic data credibly.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Identifies post-hackathon continuation opportunities, potential partners, and artifacts worth continuing.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Identifies usable datasets by scanning the data directory, identifying schemas, and highlighting gaps.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Helps teams present effectively with demo scripts, narrative structure, and judge-facing explanations.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Turns an opportunity into a concrete MVP with user flow, architecture, and scope boundaries.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Translates research into buildable solution opportunities by matching problems to solution patterns.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Helps teams choose a strong problem by analyzing problem statements, ranking opportunities, and identifying realistic directions.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Helps users navigate repository knowledge and locate specific files, datasets, or problem statements.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Reconstructs repository state by reading project artifacts, detecting the current stage, and summarizing progress.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Executes research prompts, extracts sources, and summarizes evidence for the team.

files.title: Skill: research_search
files.summary: Searches the research corpus for relevant reports and data sources matching a user query.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Prevents civic-tech mistakes by checking for hallucinated services, legal claims, GPS overclaiming, and misleading infrastructure data language.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Maps a user question to the correct section and specific research reports before attempting to answer. Uses research/index.json and CORPUS_GUIDE.md to build reading lists.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Synthesizes findings from multiple research reports with source traceability, tension identification, and gap documentation.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Answers questions using only content present in the corpus with explicit claim classification and anti-fabrication checking.
recommended_audience: agents making factual claims about Richmond

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Safely adds or updates research reports and keeps all metadata, indexes, and navigation files synchronized through a 7-step process.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Template for capturing the chosen problem, rationale, risks, and alternatives considered. Used in Phase 2 problem selection.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: Template for the project one-pager used in Phase 5 scope lock. Captures user, problem, MVP shape, data sources, AI role, limits, and demo path.

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: Template for creating a team profile file. Used by Hackbot to personalize task assignments, communication style, and role-based suggestions.
recommended_audience: teams starting their first Hackbot session

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: Template for capturing the team's working direction after Phase 1 orientation. Structures the candidate problem, likely user, and key uncertainties.

files.title: Research Corpus Index
files.summary: Machine-readable index of all 51 research reports. Each entry has id, section, title, summary, and key_terms. Use to identify relevant files before reading them.
